---
title: "AC linearization"
subtitle: "T5 · Phase T5.1 · `va-acnoise::ac`"
---

::: {.callout-note}
**Status: honestly unimplemented — a stretch goal, per `CLAUDE.md` §1, that stayed one.** The
crate compiles (`CLAUDE.md` §9's bootstrap requirement) with real type signatures and one
`#[ignore]`d test naming its own validation gate, but `run` itself is `todo!()`. This tutorial
documents the real, current state rather than describing work that hasn't happened.
:::

## Goal

Linearize a circuit about a DC operating point and sweep it over frequency — `G + jωC`, solved
per frequency point, the standard SPICE AC analysis.

## Where it fits

```
 va_core::dc::OperatingPoint ──► [ac::run] ──► AcResponse{f, x: complex per frequency}
```

`CLAUDE.md` §1 scopes AC/noise as a **stretch goal** behind DC and transient, explicitly, from
the start — this crate not shipping yet is the plan working as scoped, not a missed deadline.

## The idea (as designed, not yet built)

Every device's DC-point Jacobian already *is* the small-signal conductance matrix `G`; the
charge-channel Jacobian (`dcharge`, already stamped by every reactive `ModelInstance` for T4's
benefit) is exactly the capacitance matrix `C` this analysis would need. The intended design
reuses both without asking any device to stamp anything new — `run` would assemble
`G + jωC` at each swept frequency and solve the resulting complex linear system, the same
`ModelInstance`/`StampSink` contract every other analysis already uses.

## The code

The real, current signatures — compiling, dependency-free of any complex-number crate (a plain
`(f64, f64)` tuple stands in, so a `num-complex` type can replace it without an Interface β
change):

```rust
pub type Complex = (f64, f64);

pub struct AcSweep {
    pub fstart: f64,
    pub fstop: f64,
    pub points_per_decade: usize,
}

pub struct AcResponse {
    pub f: Vec<f64>,
    pub x: Vec<Vec<Complex>>,
}

pub fn run(_x_dc: &[f64], _sweep: AcSweep) -> Result<AcResponse, AcNoiseError> {
    todo!("T5: assemble (G + jωC), solve per frequency")
}
```

## It works

It doesn't, yet — honestly:

```console
$ cargo test -p va-acnoise
running 2 tests
test ac::tests::rc_lowpass_response ... ignored, T5: RC low-pass magnitude/phase within §7 AC band vs golden
test noise::tests::resistor_thermal_noise ... ignored, T5: resistor thermal-noise PSD matches 4kTR vs golden
```

Both ignored tests already name their own eventual validation gate (`docs/validation.md` §7's AC
band, resistor thermal noise `4kTR`) — the bar this phase will be held to once it's real, decided
up front rather than left vague.

## Limitations

Everything. `run` panics if called (`todo!()`) — there is no partial AC capability to caveat
around, just an honest, compiling stub with the right shape for when a T5 owner picks this up.

## What's next

`t5-acnoise/02-noise.qmd` — the noise analysis this AC linearization would feed.
